prompt_text:
You are shown a SKELETON view of a java repository: class headers,
inheritance, method signatures, and attributes — but NO method bodies.

Use this signature-level view to detect structural violations of Dependency Inversion Principle (DIP):
- Fat interfaces / bloated classes (many unrelated method groups)
- Clients forced to implement methods they don't need (abstract methods / NotImplementedError / empty overrides)
- Inheritance hierarchies where subclasses alter the contract shape
- Broad API...
raw_response:
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "gson/gson/src/main/java/com/google/gson/Gson.java",
      "entity_name": "Gson",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 225,
      "line_end": 253,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "The Gson class directly instantiates its internal dependency ConstructorConstructor.",
      "reasoning": "High-level modules like Gson should depend on abstractions rather than low-level implementation details. By directly calling 'new ConstructorConstructor(...)' in its constructor (L253), Gson becomes tightly coupled to a specific internal utility for object creation, violating the Dependency Inversion Principle."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "gson/gson/src/main/java/com/google/gson/GsonBuilder.java",
      "entity_name": "GsonBuilder.setDateFormat",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 638,
      "line_end": 644,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "The setDateFormat method directly instantiates the concrete SimpleDateFormat class.",
      "reasoning": "This method hard-codes a dependency on the concrete SimpleDateFormat implementation (L641) instead of depending on the DateFormat abstraction. This limits flexibility and makes it difficult to swap the formatting implementation without modifying the high-level builder logic."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "gson/gson/src/main/java/com/google/gson/internal/bind/JsonAdapterAnnotationTypeAdapterFactory.java",
      "entity_name": "JsonAdapterAnnotationTypeAdapterFactory.create",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 77,
      "line_end": 142,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "The create method directly instantiates the concrete TreeTypeAdapter class.",
      "reasoning": "The factory method creates a hard-coded dependency on the concrete TreeTypeAdapter implementation (L142) instead of using an abstraction or a provider/factory for TypeAdapters. This violates DIP by having a high-level factory module depend on a specific low-level implementation detail."
    }
  ]
}

findings.description: The Gson class directly instantiates its internal dependency ConstructorConstructor.
findings.reasoning: High-level modules like Gson should depend on abstractions rather than low-level implementation details. By directly calling 'new ConstructorConstructor(...)' in its constructor (L253), Gson becomes tightly coupled to a specific internal utility for object creation, violating the Dependency Inversion Principle.

findings.description: The setDateFormat method directly instantiates the concrete SimpleDateFormat class.
findings.reasoning: This method hard-codes a dependency on the concrete SimpleDateFormat implementation (L641) instead of depending on the DateFormat abstraction. This limits flexibility and makes it difficult to swap the formatting implementation without modifying the high-level builder logic.

findings.description: The create method directly instantiates the concrete TreeTypeAdapter class.
findings.reasoning: The factory method creates a hard-coded dependency on the concrete TreeTypeAdapter implementation (L142) instead of using an abstraction or a provider/factory for TypeAdapters. This violates DIP by having a high-level factory module depend on a specific low-level implementation detail.
